title: Human Impact on Atmosphere (Pollution, Greenhouse Gases)

flashcards.definition: A pollutant emitted directly into the atmosphere from a source (e.g., CO, SO₂, particulates from burning).
A pollutant formed by chemical reactions in the atmosphere (e.g., ground-level ozone formed from NOx + VOCs + sunlight).
Precipitation with pH below 5.6; formed when SO₂ and NOx react with water vapor; damages ecosystems and structures.
The additional warming caused by human-added greenhouse gases beyond the natural greenhouse effect.
Total greenhouse gas emissions caused by an individual, organization, event, or product, expressed as CO₂ equivalent.
